Home › Blog › Best therapist directory sites: which ones actually send clients?
Marketing

Best therapist directory sites: which ones actually send clients?

Last updated: January 15, 2025 • 5 min read

# Best therapist directory sites: which ones actually send clients? You’ve opened your private practice, polished your website, and… crickets. The biggest hurdle most therapists face isn’t clinical skill—it’s steady referrals. Directory sites promise visibility, but which ones put real clients on your calendar? I’ve tracked my own stats and polled colleagues to find out. Below you’ll find the three directories that dominate therapist conversations: Psychology Today, Headway, and Alma. I’ll show you exactly what they cost, how many clients they actually send, and who should (and shouldn’t) list on each. > **Quick verdict:** If you take insurance and practice in the US, Headway delivers the highest volume of referrals at zero marketing cost. Outside the US—or if you only take private pay—Psychology Today is still the only directory with global reach and consistent, if modest, referral flow. ## Psychology Today ### Pricing - US: $29.95/month - UK: £24.95/month - Australia: AU$45/month - Canada: CA$39/month - No commission or extra fees. ### Features - 9 million+ monthly visits globally - Instant profile activation - Built-in HIPAA-compliant telehealth video link - Client-facing blog and professional resources - Basic traffic analytics (profile views vs. clicks) ### Pros - Largest traffic of any therapist directory - Works in US, UK, Australia, Canada - Flat fee—no surprises - Listing live in minutes - Clients can call/email you directly ### Cons - High competition; new profiles can get buried fast - No insurance billing tools - No credentialing help - Analytics are minimal (you won’t know where traffic came from) ### Best for Therapists who want global reach and private-pay clients without worrying about insurance. ## Headway ### Pricing - Free to join and list - Platform keeps the difference between insurance reimbursement and your contracted rate - No monthly fee, ever ### Features - 1 million+ in-network referrals per year - End-to-end insurance billing and claims - 2–6 week credentialing turnaround - Guaranteed payment within 2 business days - Automated eligibility checks and authorizations ### Pros - Zero marketing cost - High referral volume in major metros (10–15/month average) - No billing admin - Fast, reliable payment - Credentialing support ### Cons - US only - Must accept insurance (rates are fixed) - No control over session fees - Not available outside the US ### Best for US therapists who want a steady stream of insurance clients without paperwork headaches. ## Alma ### Pricing - Free to list - Alma keeps 5–15% of insurance reimbursement (varies by state) - No monthly subscription ### Features - Insurance billing handled - 2-day payment guarantee - Peer consultation groups - Discounts on continuing-education courses - Directory placement plus internal matching ### Pros - No upfront fees - Fast payment - Built-in peer support - Insurance paneling assistance - Continuing-ed perks ### Cons - US only - Platform fee reduces take-home - Must be paneled with major insurers - Referral volume slightly lower than Headway in some regions ### Best for US clinicians seeking insurance clients plus peer support and fast payment. ## Head-to-head comparison | Feature | Psychology Today | Headway | Alma | |---------|-----------------|---------|------| | Monthly cost | $29.95–CA$39 | $0 | $0 | | Platform cut | 0% | Insurance difference | 5–15% | | Avg. referrals/month | 1–4 private pay | 10–15 insurance | 8–12 insurance | | Geographic reach | US, UK, AU, CA | US only | US only | | Insurance billing | No | Yes | Yes | | Credentialing help | No | Yes | Yes | | Peer support | No | No | Yes | ## Who should choose which - **Choose Psychology Today if** you practice outside the US, rely on private pay, or want the simplest global listing with predictable cost. - **Choose Headway if** you’re in the US, accept insurance, and want the highest referral volume without monthly fees or billing tasks. - **Choose Alma if** you’re in the US, take insurance, and value peer consultation groups and guaranteed 2-day payment over maximal referral volume. ## FAQ ### Is Psychology Today worth it for UK therapists? Yes—UK therapists report 1–3 new enquiries per month, and at £24.95 it remains the most trafficked UK-specific directory. ### How does Headway make money if it’s free? Headway keeps the spread between what insurers pay and your contracted rate; you never pay a monthly fee. ### Can I list on both Headway and Alma? Absolutely. Many therapists are paneled on both to keep schedules full; just track each platform’s fee structure. ### Which directory is best for Canadian therapists? Psychology Today is the only viable option—Headway and Alma aren’t available in Canada yet. ### Do I need to be credentialed with insurers before joining Alma? Yes, Alma requires active paneling with at least one major insurer in your state, but they assist with the paperwork. ## Conclusion If you’re outside the US, Psychology Today is still the only directory with reliable traffic. Inside the US, Headway sends the most insurance clients at zero marketing cost, while Alma adds peer support and faster payment for a small platform fee. Most busy therapists I know list on at least two of these platforms to keep caseloads steady. Ready to test the waters? Start with the directory that matches your payment model and location, then scale up. Try PSYCHOLOGY_TODAY free → Try HEADWAY free → Try ALMA free →

Reviewed by the Therapist Tools editorial team
